---
title: "project0"
output: html_document
---


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)


# CONSTANTS
CORTISOL_MAX <- 80        # Upper limit for valid Cortisol values (nmol/L)
DHEA_LIMIT <- 5.205       # Detection limit for DHEA (nmol/L)
DHEA_TOL <- 1e-6          # Tolerance for comparing DHEA to limit

df <- read.csv("Project0_Clean_v2.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)


# 2. IDENTIFY DHEA PROBLEM SUBJECTS (BEFORE ANY PROCESSING)
# Mark which DHEA values are at the detection limit
df <- df %>%
  mutate(DHEA_at_limit_raw = !is.na(DHEA..nmol.L.) & 
                              abs(DHEA..nmol.L. - DHEA_LIMIT) < DHEA_TOL)

# Find problem subjects (>25% at limit)
problem_subjects <- df %>%
  group_by(SubjectID) %>%
  summarise(
    total_dhea = sum(!is.na(DHEA..nmol.L.)),
    at_limit = sum(DHEA_at_limit_raw, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_limit = ifelse(total_dhea > 0, 100 * at_limit / total_dhea, 0),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(total_dhea > 0, pct_limit > 25) %>%
  pull(SubjectID)


# 3. TIME PARSING
parse_time <- function(x) {
  if (length(x) == 0 || is.na(x) || x == "") return(NA)
  tryCatch(parse_date_time(x, "HM", quiet = TRUE), error = function(e) NA)
}

df$Booklet_parsed <- as.POSIXct(sapply(df$Booket..Clock.Time, parse_time), 
                                 origin = "1970-01-01")
df$MEMs_parsed <- as.POSIXct(sapply(df$MEMs..Clock.Time, parse_time), 
                              origin = "1970-01-01")
df$Sleep_parsed <- as.POSIXct(sapply(df$Sleep.Diary.reported.wake.time, parse_time), 
                               origin = "1970-01-01")



# 4. CALCULATE WAKE TIMES
wake_times <- df %>%
  filter(Collection.Sample == 1) %>%
  group_by(SubjectID, Collection.Date) %>%  # FIXED: was DAYNUMB, now Collection.Date
  summarise(wake_time = first(Sleep_parsed[!is.na(Sleep_parsed)], default = NA),
            .groups = 'drop')

# Sanity check - verify each subject-date has only one wake time
wake_check <- df %>%
  left_join(wake_times, by = c("SubjectID", "Collection.Date")) %>%
  group_by(SubjectID, Collection.Date) %>%
  summarise(n_wake = n_distinct(wake_time, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  count(n_wake)

print(wake_check)
if (any(wake_check$n_wake > 1)) {
  warning("Multiple wake times per subject-date detected!")
}



# 5. CALCULATE MINUTES SINCE WAKE

df <- df %>%
  left_join(wake_times, by = c("SubjectID", "Collection.Date")) %>%  # FIXED key
  mutate(
    # Calculate time difference in minutes
    Booklet_min = as.numeric(difftime(Booklet_parsed, wake_time, units = "mins")),
    MEMs_min = as.numeric(difftime(MEMs_parsed, wake_time, units = "mins")),
    
    # Handle midnight rollover: add 24 hours (1440 min) if negative
    Booklet_min = ifelse(!is.na(Booklet_min) & Booklet_min < 0, 
                         Booklet_min + 1440, Booklet_min),
    MEMs_min = ifelse(!is.na(MEMs_min) & MEMs_min < 0, 
                      MEMs_min + 1440, MEMs_min),
    
    # Sanity check: remove impossible values (<0 or >1200 minutes)
    Booklet_min = ifelse(!is.na(Booklet_min) & (Booklet_min < 0 | Booklet_min > 1200), 
                         NA, Booklet_min),
    MEMs_min = ifelse(!is.na(MEMs_min) & (MEMs_min < 0 | MEMs_min > 1200), 
                      NA, MEMs_min)
  )


# 6. CLEAN DATA

df <- df %>%
  rename(Cortisol = Cortisol..nmol.L., DHEA = DHEA..nmol.L.)

# Save before cleaning for accurate exclusion counts
df_before_clean <- df

# Clean: remove outliers and values at detection limit
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    # Cortisol: remove values >80 nmol/L
    Cortisol = ifelse(!is.na(Cortisol) & Cortisol > CORTISOL_MAX, NA, Cortisol),
    
    # DHEA: remove values at detection limit (=5.205)
    DHEA_at_limit = !is.na(DHEA) & abs(DHEA - DHEA_LIMIT) < DHEA_TOL,
    DHEA = ifelse(DHEA_at_limit, NA, DHEA)
    
    # NOTE: We do NOT remove Subject 3037 here!
    # It will be removed only for DHEA analysis in Q3
  )

# Count exclusions from BEFORE cleaning
cort_excluded_n <- sum(!is.na(df_before_clean$Cortisol) & is.na(df$Cortisol))
dhea_limit_n <- sum(df_before_clean$DHEA_at_limit, na.rm = TRUE)


# TABLE 0: STUDY OVERVIEW & MISSING DATA

n_subjects <- n_distinct(df$SubjectID)
n_days <- n_distinct(df$DAYNUMB)
n_samples_per_day <- n_distinct(df$Collection.Sample)

# Calculate wake time missing rate only for Sample 1
# (Wake time is only recorded in Sample 1)
wake_missing <- sum(is.na(df$Sleep_parsed[df$Collection.Sample == 1]))
wake_total <- sum(df$Collection.Sample == 1)
wake_pct <- 100 * wake_missing / wake_total

table0 <- data.frame(
  Variable = c(
    "Study Design:",
    "  Subjects (n)",
    "  Days per subject",
    "  Samples per day",
    "  Total observations",
    "",
    "Time Variables:",
    "  Collection Date",
    "  Booklet clock time",
    "  MEMs clock time",
    "  Sleep Diary wake time (Sample 1 only)",  # CLARIFIED
    "  Booklet minutes since wake",
    "  MEMs minutes since wake",
    "",
    "Outcome Variables:",
    "  Cortisol (nmol/L)",
    "  DHEA (nmol/L)"
  ),
  Total_N = c(
    "", as.character(n_subjects), as.character(n_days), 
    as.character(n_samples_per_day), as.character(nrow(df)),
    "",
    "", 
    as.character(nrow(df)),  # Collection Date
    as.character(nrow(df)),  # Booklet clock time
    as.character(nrow(df)),  # MEMs clock time
    as.character(wake_total),  # Sleep Diary (only Sample 1)
    as.character(nrow(df)),  # Booklet minutes
    as.character(nrow(df)),  # MEMs minutes
    "",
    "", 
    as.character(nrow(df)),  # Cortisol
    as.character(nrow(df))   # DHEA
  ),
  Missing_N = c(
    "", "", "", "", "",
    "",
    "",
    as.character(sum(is.na(df$Collection.Date))),
    as.character(sum(is.na(df$Booklet_parsed))),
    as.character(sum(is.na(df$MEMs_parsed))),
    as.character(wake_missing),  # FIXED
    as.character(sum(is.na(df$Booklet_min))),
    as.character(sum(is.na(df$MEMs_min))),
    "",
    "",
    as.character(sum(is.na(df$Cortisol))),
    as.character(sum(is.na(df$DHEA)))
  ),
  Complete_N = c(
    "", "", "", "", "",
    "",
    "",
    as.character(sum(!is.na(df$Collection.Date))),
    as.character(sum(!is.na(df$Booklet_parsed))),
    as.character(sum(!is.na(df$MEMs_parsed))),
    as.character(wake_total - wake_missing),  # FIXED
    as.character(sum(!is.na(df$Booklet_min))),
    as.character(sum(!is.na(df$MEMs_min))),
    "",
    "",
    as.character(sum(!is.na(df$Cortisol))),
    as.character(sum(!is.na(df$DHEA)))
  ),
  Missing_Percent = c(
    "", "", "", "", "",
    "",
    "",
    sprintf("%.1f", 100 * sum(is.na(df$Collection.Date)) / nrow(df)),
    sprintf("%.1f", 100 * sum(is.na(df$Booklet_parsed)) / nrow(df)),
    sprintf("%.1f", 100 * sum(is.na(df$MEMs_parsed)) / nrow(df)),
    sprintf("%.1f", wake_pct),  # FIXED
    sprintf("%.1f", 100 * sum(is.na(df$Booklet_min)) / nrow(df)),
    sprintf("%.1f", 100 * sum(is.na(df$MEMs_min)) / nrow(df)),
    "",
    "",
    sprintf("%.1f", 100 * sum(is.na(df$Cortisol)) / nrow(df)),
    sprintf("%.1f", 100 * sum(is.na(df$DHEA)) / nrow(df))
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

print(knitr::kable(table0, format = "simple",
                    col.names = c("Variable", "Total_N", "Missing_N", "Complete_N", "Missing_Percent")))

# Auto-detect anomalies instead of hardcoding

write.csv(table0, "Table0_Study_Overview.csv", row.names = FALSE)



# QUESTION 1: AGREEMENT
# Prepare Q1 data: need both Booklet and MEMs times
df_q1 <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Booklet_min) & !is.na(MEMs_min)) %>%
  mutate(
    SubjectID = factor(SubjectID),
    difference = Booklet_min - MEMs_min,
    mean_time = (Booklet_min + MEMs_min) / 2
  )

# Fit linear mixed model: Booklet ~ MEMs + (1|Subject)
m1 <- lmer(Booklet_min ~ MEMs_min + (1|SubjectID), data = df_q1, REML = TRUE)

beta <- fixef(m1)
coef_summary <- summary(m1)$coefficients

ci <- confint(m1, method = "Wald", parm = "beta_")
ci_int <- ci["(Intercept)", ]
ci_slope <- ci["MEMs_min", ]

# Test if intercept = 0 (perfect agreement would have intercept = 0)
test_int0 <- lmerTest::contest1D(m1, L = c(1, 0), rhs = 0)
t_int <- as.numeric(test_int0["t value"])
p_int <- as.numeric(test_int0["Pr(>|t|)"])

# Test if slope = 1 (perfect agreement would have slope = 1)
test_slope1 <- lmerTest::contest1D(m1, L = c(0, 1), rhs = 1)
t_slope1 <- as.numeric(test_slope1["t value"])
p_slope1 <- as.numeric(test_slope1["Pr(>|t|)"])

# Create results table
q1_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = c("Intercept", "Slope"),
  Estimate = c(beta["(Intercept)"], beta["MEMs_min"]),
  SE = c(coef_summary["(Intercept)", "Std. Error"], 
         coef_summary["MEMs_min", "Std. Error"]),
  CI_Lower = c(ci_int[1], ci_slope[1]),
  CI_Upper = c(ci_int[2], ci_slope[2]),
  Perfect_Agreement = c(0, 1),
  t_value = c(t_int, t_slope1),
  p_value = c(p_int, p_slope1),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)
rownames(q1_results) <- NULL

# Calculate Bland-Altman statistics
mean_diff <- mean(df_q1$difference, na.rm = TRUE)
sd_diff <- sd(df_q1$difference, na.rm = TRUE)
loa_upper <- mean_diff + 1.96 * sd_diff  # Upper limit of agreement
loa_lower <- mean_diff - 1.96 * sd_diff  # Lower limit of agreement

ba_summary <- data.frame(
  Metric = c("Mean difference (Booklet - MEMs)", 
             "SD of differences",
             "95% Limit of Agreement (Lower)", 
             "95% Limit of Agreement (Upper)"),
  Value = c(mean_diff, sd_diff, loa_lower, loa_upper)
)

print(knitr::kable(q1_results, format = "simple", digits = 3))
print(knitr::kable(ba_summary, format = "simple", digits = 2))

write.csv(q1_results, "Table1_Q1_Agreement.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(ba_summary, "Table1b_BlandAltman.csv", row.names = FALSE)



# QUESTION 2: ADHERENCE
# Prepare adherence data (only observations with valid MEMs time)
df_adherence <- df %>%
  filter(Collection.Sample %in% c(2, 4)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(MEMs_min)) %>%
  mutate(
    # Target times: Sample 2 = 30 min, Sample 4 = 600 min after wake
    Target_Time = case_when(
      Collection.Sample == 2 ~ 30,
      Collection.Sample == 4 ~ 600
    ),
    Deviation = MEMs_min - Target_Time,
    Abs_Deviation = abs(Deviation),
    Within_7.5min = Abs_Deviation <= 7.5,    # Strict window
    Within_15min = Abs_Deviation <= 15,      # Loose window
    Sample_Label = case_when(
      Collection.Sample == 2 ~ "+30 min",
      Collection.Sample == 4 ~ "+10 hours"
    )
  )

# Calculate adherence with exact binomial confidence intervals
calc_adherence_with_ci <- function(data, sample_num, window_min) {
  subset_data <- data %>% filter(Collection.Sample == sample_num)
  n <- nrow(subset_data)
  
  # Count how many are within the time window
  if (window_min == 7.5) {
    x <- sum(subset_data$Within_7.5min)
  } else {
    x <- sum(subset_data$Within_15min)
  }
  
  # Exact binomial CI
  ci <- binom.test(x, n, conf.level = 0.95)
  
  return(data.frame(
    Sample = ifelse(sample_num == 2, "+30 min", "+10 hours"),
    Target_Time = ifelse(sample_num == 2, "30 min", "600 min"),
    Window = paste0("±", window_min, " min"),
    N_Total = n,
    N_Adherent = x,
    Percentage = round(100 * x / n, 1),
    CI_Lower = round(100 * ci$conf.int[1], 1),
    CI_Upper = round(100 * ci$conf.int[2], 1)
  ))
}

# Calculate for all combinations: 2 samples × 2 windows = 4 rows
q2_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  calc_adherence_with_ci(df_adherence, 2, 7.5),
  calc_adherence_with_ci(df_adherence, 2, 15),
  calc_adherence_with_ci(df_adherence, 4, 7.5),
  calc_adherence_with_ci(df_adherence, 4, 15)
)

print(knitr::kable(q2_results, format = "simple", digits = 1))

write.csv(q2_results, "Table2_Q2_Adherence.csv", row.names = FALSE)



# QUESTION 3: HORMONE PATTERNS
# Prepare base Q3 data - require BOTH booklet and cap times
data_q3 <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Booklet_min) & !is.na(MEMs_min)) %>%  # Both required
  mutate(
    booklet = Booklet_min,
    cap = MEMs_min,
    
    # Friend's piecewise variables
    t1_30 = pmin(cap, 30),
    t2_30 = pmax(cap - 30, 0),
    
    id = factor(SubjectID)
  )

# Cortisol: Use ALL subjects (including any problem subjects)
data_q3_cort <- data_q3 %>%
  filter(!is.na(Cortisol) & Cortisol > 0) %>%
  mutate(log_cort = log(Cortisol))

# DHEA: EXCLUDE problem subjects (e.g., Subject 3037)
# This is where we finally remove Subject 3037!
data_q3_dhea <- data_q3 %>%
  filter(!is.na(DHEA) & DHEA > 0) %>%
  filter(!(SubjectID %in% problem_subjects)) %>%  # Exclude here!
  mutate(log_dhea = log(DHEA))

# Fit piecewise linear mixed models with log-transformed outcomes
cort_fit_30 <- lmer(log_cort ~ t1_30 + t2_30 + (1 | id), 
                    data = data_q3_cort, REML = TRUE)
dhea_fit_30 <- lmer(log_dhea ~ t1_30 + t2_30 + (1 | id), 
                    data = data_q3_dhea, REML = TRUE)

# Extract summaries
cort_summary <- summary(cort_fit_30)
dhea_summary <- summary(dhea_fit_30)

# Calculate ICC (Intraclass Correlation Coefficient)
# ICC = between-subject variance / total variance
calc_icc <- function(model) {
  vc <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(model))
  var_subject <- vc$vcov[1]
  var_resid <- vc$vcov[2]
  icc <- var_subject / (var_subject + var_resid)
  return(round(100 * icc, 1))
}

icc_cort <- calc_icc(cort_fit_30)
icc_dhea <- calc_icc(dhea_fit_30)

# Get variance components
vc_cort <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(cort_fit_30))
vc_dhea <- as.data.frame(VarCorr(dhea_fit_30))

# Create results table in side-by-side format
q3_results <- data.frame(
  Parameter = c(
    "Fixed Effects",
    "  Intercept (at waking)",
    "  Time1 (0-30 min slope)",
    "  Time2 (post-30 min slope)",
    "",
    "Random Effects",
    "  Subject variance",
    "  Residual variance",
    "  ICC"
  ),
  log_Cortisol = c(
    "",
    sprintf("%.3f (p %s)", 
            fixef(cort_fit_30)["(Intercept)"],
            ifelse(cort_summary$coefficients["(Intercept)", "Pr(>|t|)"] < 0.001, 
                   "< 0.001",
                   sprintf("= %.3f", cort_summary$coefficients["(Intercept)", "Pr(>|t|)"]))),
    sprintf("%.4f (p = %.3f)", 
            fixef(cort_fit_30)["t1_30"],
            cort_summary$coefficients["t1_30", "Pr(>|t|)"]),
    sprintf("%.4f (p %s)", 
            fixef(cort_fit_30)["t2_30"],
            ifelse(cort_summary$coefficients["t2_30", "Pr(>|t|)"] < 0.001,
                   "< 0.001",
                   sprintf("= %.3f", cort_summary$coefficients["t2_30", "Pr(>|t|)"]))),
    "",
    "",
    sprintf("%.3f", vc_cort$vcov[1]),
    sprintf("%.3f", vc_cort$vcov[2]),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", icc_cort)
  ),
  log_DHEA = c(
    "",
    sprintf("%.3f (p = %.3f)", 
            fixef(dhea_fit_30)["(Intercept)"],
            dhea_summary$coefficients["(Intercept)", "Pr(>|t|)"]),
    sprintf("%.4f (p %s)", 
            fixef(dhea_fit_30)["t1_30"],
            ifelse(dhea_summary$coefficients["t1_30", "Pr(>|t|)"] < 0.001,
                   "< 0.001",
                   sprintf("= %.3f", dhea_summary$coefficients["t1_30", "Pr(>|t|)"]))),
    sprintf("%.4f (p %s)", 
            fixef(dhea_fit_30)["t2_30"],
            ifelse(dhea_summary$coefficients["t2_30", "Pr(>|t|)"] < 0.001,
                   "< 0.001",
                   sprintf("= %.3f", dhea_summary$coefficients["t2_30", "Pr(>|t|)"]))),
    "",
    "",
    sprintf("%.3f", vc_dhea$vcov[1]),
    sprintf("%.3f", vc_dhea$vcov[2]),
    sprintf("%.1f%%", icc_dhea)
  ),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)


print(knitr::kable(q3_results, format = "simple",
                    col.names = c("Parameter", "log(Cortisol)", "log(DHEA)")))

write.csv(q3_results, "Table3_Q3_Hormones.csv", row.names = FALSE)


# FIGURES
# Figure 1: Bland-Altman plot
plot(df_q1$mean_time, df_q1$difference,
     xlab = "Mean of Booklet and MEMs (minutes)", 
     ylab = "Difference: Booklet - MEMs (minutes)",
     main = "Bland-Altman Plot",
     pch = 16, col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.3))
abline(h = mean_diff, col = "blue", lwd = 2)          # Mean difference
abline(h = loa_upper, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)  # Upper LoA
abline(h = loa_lower, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2)  # Lower LoA
abline(h = 0, col = "gray", lty = 3)                  # Zero line
legend("topright",
       c(sprintf("Mean: %.1f", mean_diff),
         sprintf("Upper LoA: %.1f", loa_upper),
         sprintf("Lower LoA: %.1f", loa_lower)),
       col = c("blue", "red", "red"), lty = c(1, 2, 2), lwd = 2, bty = "n", cex = 0.8)

# Figure 2: Combined Hormone Diurnal Patterns (side by side)
par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1))

# Panel A: Cortisol (use MEMs time on x-axis)
plot(data_q3_cort$cap, data_q3_cort$log_cort,
     xlab = "MEMs minutes since wake", 
     ylab = "log(Cortisol) [log(nmol/L)]",
     main = "A. Cortisol Diurnal Pattern",
     pch = 16, col = rgb(0, 0, 1, 0.3),
     xlim = c(0, max(data_q3_cort$cap, na.rm=TRUE)))

# Piecewise fitted line (use cap for prediction)
time_seq_cort <- seq(0, max(data_q3_cort$cap, na.rm=TRUE), length.out=200)
pred_cort <- fixef(cort_fit_30)["(Intercept)"] + 
             fixef(cort_fit_30)["t1_30"] * pmin(time_seq_cort, 30) + 
             fixef(cort_fit_30)["t2_30"] * pmax(time_seq_cort - 30, 0)
lines(time_seq_cort, pred_cort, col = "red", lwd = 2)
abline(v = 30, col = "gray", lty = 2)  # Mark the knot at 30 min
text(30, par("usr")[4]*0.95, "30 min", col = "gray", pos = 4, cex = 0.8)

# Panel B: DHEA (use MEMs time on x-axis)
plot(data_q3_dhea$cap, data_q3_dhea$log_dhea,
     xlab = "MEMs minutes since wake", 
     ylab = "log(DHEA) [log(nmol/L)]",
     main = "B. DHEA Diurnal Pattern",
     pch = 16, col = rgb(0, 0.5, 0, 0.3),
     xlim = c(0, max(data_q3_dhea$cap, na.rm=TRUE)))

# Piecewise fitted line (use cap for prediction)
time_seq_dhea <- seq(0, max(data_q3_dhea$cap, na.rm=TRUE), length.out=200)
pred_dhea <- fixef(dhea_fit_30)["(Intercept)"] + 
             fixef(dhea_fit_30)["t1_30"] * pmin(time_seq_dhea, 30) + 
             fixef(dhea_fit_30)["t2_30"] * pmax(time_seq_dhea - 30, 0)
lines(time_seq_dhea, pred_dhea, col = "red", lwd = 2)
abline(v = 30, col = "gray", lty = 2)  # Mark the knot at 30 min
text(30, par("usr")[4]*0.95, "30 min", col = "gray", pos = 4, cex = 0.8)
```
```
